ucs.title: [LLM] Mustang Panda PlugX/ToneShell DLL sideloading via abused signed vendor binaries
ucs.description: Hunts the actor's signature execution chain: a legitimately-signed third-party binary (Adobe, ESET, Avast, McAfee, Symantec, Norman, Kaspersky) executed from a non-standard path (Public, ProgramData, AppData, Temp, USB) that loads an unsigned DLL from the same directory — the canonical Mustang Panda / RedDelta / Earth Preta PlugX, ToneShell, and PUBLOAD sideloading pattern documented by Trend Micro, Recorded Future, ESET, and Mandiant.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("AcroRd32.exe","Acrobat.exe","AvastSvc.exe","AvastBrowser.exe","ekrn.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","RasTls.exe","SymantecHostIDS.exe","NisSrv.exe","gup.exe","avp.exe","AAM Updates Notifier.exe","hkcmd.exe","DLPDoctor.exe","ZAM.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe")) AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","*\\Windows\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as loaded_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND NOT (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Windows\\System32\\*","*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*","*\\Program Files\\*")) by Filesystem.process_guid | rename Filesystem.process_guid as process_guid] | where mvcount(loaded_dll) > 0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let SuspectBins = dynamic(["acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","avastsvc.exe","avastbrowser.exe","ekrn.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","rastls.exe","symantechostids.exe","nissrv.exe","gup.exe","avp.exe","aam updates notifier.exe","hkcmd.exe","dlpdoctor.exe","zam.exe","potplayermini.exe","googleupdate.exe"]);
let SuspectPaths = dynamic([@"c:\users\public\",@"c:\programdata\",@"c:\users\",@"c:\windows\temp\",@"c:\perflogs\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (SuspectBins)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (SuspectPaths) or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex @"^[d-z]:\\"
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\" and FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath) == tolower(FolderPath)
| extend SignerOk = iff(InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus == "Valid", true, false)
| where SignerOk == true
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | project DeviceId, InitiatingProcessSHA256=SHA256, ParentProcessName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine) on InitiatingProcessSHA256
ucs.rationale: Mustang Panda has built nearly every PlugX variant they ship (THOR, RedDelta variant, ToneShell, PUBLOAD, MISTCLOAK) around DLL search-order hijacking using a small, repeated set of signed AV/Adobe/Razer/Norman binaries that they rename or copy to writable directories. Generic sideload hunts fire on InstallShield/AnyDesk noise; this UC pins to (1) the specific binaries Mustang Panda has been obser

ucs.title: [LLM] Mustang Panda HIUPAN/MISTCLOAK USB worm propagation (hidden files + renamed signed loader on removable media)
ucs.description: Detects the actor's USB self-propagation chain (HIUPAN, MISTCLOAK, U2EC) used heavily against APAC government, NGO, and Vatican-aligned targets: a process executing from a removable drive that hides folders via attrib +s +h, drops or copies a renamed legitimate binary plus DLL to the volume root, and creates a decoy LNK pointing back into a hidden directory.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_path="[D-Z]:\\*" AND (Processes.process_name="attrib.exe" OR Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" OR Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wscript.exe") AND (Processes.process="*+s +h*" OR Processes.process="*attrib*+h*" OR Processes.process="*\\RECYCLER.BIN*" OR Processes.process="* /c start *\\*.lnk*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_guid Processes.parent_process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as drop_files values(Filesystem.file_path) as drop_paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path="[D-Z]:\\*" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_guid] | where mvcount(drop_files) >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let RemovableDrive = @"^[d-z]:\\";
let HideCmd = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "attrib.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("+s","+h") or ProcessCommandLine has "+h +s"
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex RemovableDrive or InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex RemovableDrive
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let UsbDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FolderPath matches regex RemovableDrive
| where FileName endswith ".lnk" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".exe"
| summarize DroppedFiles = make_set(FileName), DropPaths = make_set(FolderPath), DropCount = dcount(FileName) by DeviceId, bin(Timestamp, 10m), InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName
| where DropCount >= 2 and DroppedFiles has_any (".lnk") and (DroppedFiles has_any (".dll") or DroppedFiles has_any (".exe"));
HideCmd
| join kind=inner (UsbDrops) on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath matches regex RemovableDrive
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".lnk","rundll32","\\RECYCLER")
    | project DeviceId, ChildExec=ProcessCommandLine, ChildImage=FolderPath, ChildSHA=SHA256
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, ProcessCommandLine, DroppedFiles, DropPaths, ChildExec, ChildImage, ChildSHA, InitiatingProcessSHA256
ucs.rationale: USB-borne propagation is one of Mustang Panda's most distinctive 2022–2024 traits — Trend Micro's Earth Preta reporting, ESET's HIUPAN coverage, and Palo Alto's Stately Taurus reporting all describe the same triad: process running from a removable drive, attrib +s +h hiding a RECYCLER-style folder, then dropping LNK + renamed signed loader + DLL to the volume root. Most APTs do not pivot via USB w
